tasks.title: Create database schema and migrations
tasks.description: Create the initial database schema including users, sessions, and login_attempts tables. Write SQL migration files with proper indexes and constraints. Ensure UUID generation and timestamp defaults are configured correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Users table has proper constraints on email (unique, not null)
Sessions table references users with ON DELETE CASCADE
Indexes created on frequently queried columns (email, user_id)
Migration files are idempotent (can be run multiple times safely)

tasks.title: Implement User model with password hashing
tasks.description: Create the User model class with bcrypt password hashing (cost factor 12). Include email validation using regex pattern. Add methods for password verification using constant-time comparison.
acceptance_criteria: Password hashing uses bcrypt with cost factor 12
Email validation rejects invalid formats
Password verification is constant-time to prevent timing attacks
Model methods handle edge cases (empty strings, None values)

tasks.title: Implement AuthService with JWT generation
tasks.description: Create AuthService class with register, login, refresh, and logout methods. Implement JWT token generation with configurable expiration. Use refresh tokens for session management.
acceptance_criteria: JWT tokens include user_id and expiration claims
Access tokens expire in 15 minutes (configurable)
Refresh tokens are cryptographically secure (32+ bytes)
Logout invalidates the refresh token

tasks.title: Implement SessionService for session management
tasks.description: Create SessionService to manage user sessions including creation, validation, and revocation. Store session metadata (device info, IP address, user agent). Implement sliding expiration.
acceptance_criteria: Sessions store device and IP metadata
Sliding expiration extends session on activity
Users can view and revoke their sessions
Expired sessions are properly handled

tasks.title: Implement rate limiter middleware
tasks.description: Create rate limiting middleware using sliding window algorithm. Implement per-IP and per-user rate limits. Add Retry-After header when rate limited. Support configurable limits.
acceptance_criteria: Rate limiter uses sliding window algorithm
Returns 429 status with Retry-After header
Supports both IP-based and user-based limits
Limits are configurable via environment variables

tasks.title: Create auth API endpoints
tasks.description: Implement REST API endpoints for registration, login, token refresh, logout, and user info. Apply rate limiting to login and registration endpoints. Validate request inputs.
acceptance_criteria: All endpoints validate input before processing
Login and registration have rate limiting applied
Error responses use consistent format
Endpoints return appropriate HTTP status codes

tasks.title: Write unit tests for auth services
tasks.description: Create comprehensive unit tests for User model, AuthService, and SessionService. Test edge cases, error handling, and security features. Mock database connections.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover all public methods
Edge cases (invalid inputs, expired tokens) are tested
Security features (hashing, timing) are verified
Test coverage is at least 80%

tasks.title: Write integration tests for API endpoints
tasks.description: Create integration tests that exercise the full auth flow through the API. Test registration, login, refresh, and logout sequences. Verify rate limiting behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Full auth flow is tested end-to-end
Rate limiting triggers after threshold
Concurrent requests are handled correctly
Error responses match expected format

clarifications_needed: Should email verification be blocking (user cannot login until verified) or non-blocking?
What is the maximum number of active sessions per user (or unlimited)?
Should rate limiting be stored in Redis or is in-memory acceptable for single-instance deployments?
